query: Select only customer ID, name, and email columns from the dataset
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
selected = select_pipe | Select('customer_id', 'name', 'email')
result_df = selected.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Data Privacy & Focused Analysis - Extract only essential customer information for specific business purposes while maintaining data privacy compliance and reducing data exposure for targeted communications or analysis.
natural_language_questions: Can you get just the customer contact information?
I only need names and emails for the campaign
Can you extract basic customer details?
What are the essential customer fields?

configuration_hints.columns: List specific column names as strings or use column selectors
configuration_hints.privacy: Only select columns needed for specific business purpose
configuration_hints.data_minimization: Follow principle of least privilege for data access

typical_parameters.performance: Selecting fewer columns improves memory usage and processing speed

query: Select all numeric columns for statistical analysis
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
numeric_data = select_pipe | select_numeric()
result_df = numeric_data.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Quantitative Analysis & Machine Learning - Focus analysis on numerical data for statistical modeling, correlation analysis, and machine learning applications where only quantitative features are relevant.
natural_language_questions: Can you get all the numerical data?
I want to analyze just the numbers
What are the quantitative columns?
Can you select numeric fields for analysis?

configuration_hints.automatic_detection: Automatically identifies int, float, and numeric data types
configuration_hints.excludes: Filters out text, categorical, and date columns
configuration_hints.downstream_use: Perfect for correlation analysis and machine learning

query: Select all string columns for text analysis
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
text_data = select_pipe | select_strings()
result_df = text_data.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Text Analytics & Natural Language Processing - Isolate text-based data for sentiment analysis, content categorization, keyword extraction, and other NLP applications in customer feedback or content analysis.
natural_language_questions: Can you get all the text columns?
I want to analyze customer comments
What are the text-based fields?
Can you select string data for NLP?

configuration_hints.automatic_detection: Identifies string/object columns that contain text
configuration_hints.preprocessing: May need cleaning before text analysis

query: Select all date and time columns for temporal analysis
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
time_data = select_pipe | select_dates()
result_df = time_data.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Time Series Analysis & Temporal Insights - Focus on temporal data for trend analysis, seasonality detection, and time-based business intelligence to understand patterns over time and forecast future trends.
natural_language_questions: Can you get all the date columns?
I want to analyze trends over time
What are the temporal fields?
Can you select time-based data?

configuration_hints.automatic_detection: Identifies datetime, date, and time data types

query: Select columns containing 'score' in their name
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
score_cols = select_pipe | select_by_pattern('.*score.*')
result_df = score_cols.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Performance Metrics & KPI Analysis - Extract performance indicators and scoring metrics for business evaluation, customer assessment, and quality measurement across different business dimensions.
natural_language_questions: Can you get all the score columns?
I want all performance metrics
What columns measure performance?
Can you find rating and scoring fields?

configuration_hints.pattern: Use regex patterns for flexible matching (.*score.*, rating_*, *_index)

query: Remove sensitive columns like password and SSN from the dataset
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
clean_data = select_pipe | Deselect('password', 'ssn', 'credit_card')
result_df = clean_data.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Data Security & Privacy Compliance - Remove personally identifiable information (PII) and sensitive data to ensure compliance with privacy regulations and reduce security risks in data analysis and sharing.
natural_language_questions: Can you remove sensitive information?
I need to exclude PII data
Can you strip out confidential fields?
How do I make this data safe to share?

configuration_hints.security_focus: Identify and remove all PII and sensitive data
configuration_hints.compliance: Consider GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulations
configuration_hints.documentation: Document what data was removed for audit purposes

typical_parameters.verification: Review remaining columns for hidden sensitive data

query: Rename columns to standardize naming conventions
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
renamed = select_pipe | Rename({
    'user_id': 'customer_id',
    'first_name': 'first',
    'last_name': 'last',
    'total_spent': 'spending'
})
result_df = renamed.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Data Standardization & Integration - Establish consistent naming conventions across datasets to improve data integration, reduce confusion, and enable seamless collaboration across teams and systems.
natural_language_questions: Can you standardize the column names?
I need consistent naming across datasets
Can you rename these columns?
How do I fix inconsistent column names?

configuration_hints.mapping_dict: Use dictionary with old_name: new_name format
configuration_hints.consistency: Use snake_case, camelCase, or chosen convention consistently

typical_parameters.validation: Verify renamed columns maintain business meaning

query: Add computed columns for revenue and profit calculations
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
enhanced = select_pipe | AddColumns({
    'revenue': lambda df: df['quantity'] * df['price'],
    'profit': lambda df: (df['quantity'] * df['price']) - (df['quantity'] * df['cost']),
    'profit_margin': lambda df: ((df['quantity'] * df['price']) - (df['quantity'] * df['cost'])) / (df['quantity'] * df['price'])
})
result_df = enhanced.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Business Intelligence & KPI Creation - Generate calculated metrics and key performance indicators from raw data to support business analysis, financial reporting, and strategic decision-making.
natural_language_questions: Can you calculate revenue from price and quantity?
I need to add profit calculations
Can you create KPI columns?
How do I add calculated fields?

configuration_hints.error_handling: Consider division by zero and null value handling

query: Select feature columns for machine learning model training
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
features = (select_pipe
    | select_numeric()
    | Select(contains('feature'), contains('metric'), contains('score'))
    | Deselect(contains('target'), contains('label'), contains('id'))
)
result_df = features.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Machine Learning Feature Preparation - Systematically prepare feature datasets for model training by selecting relevant predictive variables while excluding target variables and identifiers that could cause data leakage.
natural_language_questions: Can you prepare features for machine learning?
What columns should I use for modeling?
Can you get predictive variables only?
How do I select ML features?

configuration_hints.feature_types: Focus on numeric features for most ML algorithms
configuration_hints.exclude_targets: Remove target variables to prevent data leakage
configuration_hints.exclude_ids: Remove identifiers that don't have predictive value

query: Clean and prepare data by removing unwanted columns and standardizing names
example:
select_pipe = SelectPipe.from_dataframe(data)
clean_data = (select_pipe
    | Deselect(contains('temp'), contains('debug'), contains('internal'))
    | Rename({
        'user_id': 'customer_id',
        'first_name': 'first',
        'last_name': 'last'
    })
    | Reorder('customer_id', 'first', 'last', 'email')
)
result_df = clean_data.to_df()

instructions.business_case: Data Quality & Production Readiness - Clean and standardize datasets for business use by removing temporary fields, standardizing naming conventions, and organizing columns for optimal usability and consistency.
natural_language_questions: Can you clean up this dataset?
I need to prepare data for production
Can you standardize and organize columns?
How do I make this data business-ready?

configuration_hints.remove_clutter: Eliminate temporary, debug, and internal columns
configuration_hints.logical_order: Arrange columns in logical business order
